to.p: make an order to be made under this section with respect to a child

condition.p: receiving the application of a local authority or authorised person for the order
it is satisfied that—

ul.li: (a) the applicant has reasonable cause to suspect that the child is suffering,
or is likely to suffer,
significant harm;
(b) an assessment of the state of the child’s health or development,
or of the way in which he has been treated,
is required to enable the applicant to determine whether or not the child is suffering,
or is likely to suffer,
significant harm;
and (c) it is unlikely that such an assessment will be made,
or be satisfactory,
in the absence of an order under this section.

to.p: treat an application under this section as an application for an emergency protection order.
make a child assessment order

condition.p: it is satisfied—

ul.li: (a) that there are grounds for making an emergency protection order with respect to the child;
and (b) that it ought to make such an order rather than a child assessment order.
(a) specify the date by which the assessment is to begin;
and (b) have effect for such period,
not exceeding 7 days beginning with that date,
as may be specified in the order.

for.p: any person who is in a position to produce a child

ul.li: (a) to produce him to such person as may be named in the order;
and (b) to comply with such directions relating to the assessment of the child as the court thinks fit to specify in the order.

condition.p: a child assessment order is in force with respect to the child

for.p: any person carrying out the assessment, or any part of it

to.p: do so in accordance with the terms of the order

condition.p: a child assessment order is in force

to.p: refuse to submit to a medical or psychiatric examination or other assessment

condition.p: the child is of sufficient understanding to make an informed decision

to.p: be kept away from home

condition.p: this is

ul.li: (a) in accordance with directions specified in the order;
(b) necessary for the purposes of the assessment;
and (c) for such period or periods as may be specified in the order.

to.p: contain directions regarding contact the child must be allowed to have with other persons while away from home

condition.p: the child is to be kept away from home.

for.p: any person making an application for a child assessment order

to.p: take such steps as are reasonably practicable to ensure that notice of the application is given to—

ul.li: (a) the child’s parents;
(b) any person who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him;
(c) any other person caring for the child;
(d) any person named in a child arrangements order as a person with whom the child is to spend time or otherwise have contact;
(e) any person who is allowed to have contact with the child by virtue of an order under section 34;
and (f) the child,

condition.p: the hearing of the application

to.p: make provision as to the circumstances in which—

ul.li: (a) any of the persons mentioned in subsection (11);
or (b) such other person as may be specified in the rules,
may apply to the court for a child assessment order to be varied or discharged.

to.p: make an order to be made under this section with respect to a child

condition.p: any person (“the applicant”) applies to the court for the order
it is satisfied that—

ul.li: (a) there is reasonable cause to believe that the child is likely to suffer significant harm if— (i) he is not removed to accommodation provided by or on behalf of the applicant;
or (ii) he does not remain in the place in which he is then being accommodated;
(b) in the case of an application made by a local authority— (i) enquiries are being made with respect to the child under section 47(1)(b);

and (ii) those enquiries are being frustrated by access to the child being unreasonably refused to a person authorised to seek access and that the applicant has reasonable cause to believe that access to the child is required as a matter of urgency;
or (c) in the case of an application made by an authorised person— (i) the applicant has reasonable cause to suspect that a child is suffering,
or is likely to suffer,
significant harm;
(ii) the applicant is making enquiries with respect to the child’s welfare;

and (iii) those enquiries are being frustrated by access to the child being unreasonably refused to a person authorised to seek access and the applicant has reasonable cause to believe that access to the child is required as a matter of urgency.

for.p: any person seeking access to a child in connection with enquiries under section 44(1) and purporting to be authorised

to.p: produce duly authenticated evidence of authorisation

condition.p: asked to do so

for.p: any person in a position to do so

to.p: comply with a request to produce the child to the applicant

condition.p: an emergency protection order is in force

to.p: remove the child to accommodation provided by or on behalf of the applicant and keep the child there, or prevent the child’s removal from a hospital or other place

condition.p: an emergency protection order is in force

to.p: exercise the removal or prevention powers under subsection (4)(b)

condition.p: it is to safeguard the welfare of the child

to.p: take action in meeting parental responsibility for the child

condition.p: it is reasonably required to safeguard or promote the welfare of the child (having regard to the duration of the order)

to.p: comply with any regulations made by the Secretary of State for the purposes of this subsection

condition.p: an emergency protection order is in force

to.p: refuse to submit to a medical or psychiatric examination or other assessment

condition.p: a direction for such examination or assessment is given under subsection (6)(b) and the child is of sufficient understanding

to.p: contain such directions as the court thinks fit with regard to the contact that he must be allowed to have with other persons while away from home

condition.p: a child is to be kept away from home.

for.p: Any person making an application for a child assessment order

to.p: take such steps as are reasonably practicable to ensure that notice of the application is given to—

ul.li: (a) the child’s parents;
(b) any person who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him;
(c) any other person caring for the child;
(d) any person named in a child arrangements order as a person with whom the child is to spend time or otherwise have contact;
(e) any person who is allowed to have contact with the child by virtue of an order under section 34;
and (f) the child,

condition.p: the hearing of the application.

to.p: make provision as to the circumstances in which—

ul.li: (a) any of the persons mentioned in subsection (11);
or (b) such other person as may be specified in the rules,
may apply to the court for a child assessment order to be varied or discharged.

to.p: make an order to be made under this section with respect to a child

condition.p: any person (“the applicant”) applies to the court for the order
it is satisfied that—

ul.li: (a) there is reasonable cause to believe that the child is likely to suffer significant harm if— (i) he is not removed to accommodation provided by or on behalf of the applicant;
or (ii) he does not remain in the place in which he is then being accommodated;
(b) in the case of an application made by a local authority— (i) enquiries are being made with respect to the child under section 47(1)(b);

and (ii) those enquiries are being frustrated by access to the child being unreasonably refused to a person authorised to seek access and that the applicant has reasonable cause to believe that access to the child is required as a matter of urgency;
or (c) in the case of an application made by an authorised person— (i) the applicant has reasonable cause to suspect that a child is suffering,
or is likely to suffer,
significant harm;
(ii) the applicant is making enquiries with respect to the child’s welfare;

and (iii) those enquiries are being frustrated by access to the child being unreasonably refused to a person authorised to seek access and the applicant has reasonable cause to believe that access to the child is required as a matter of urgency.

to.p: produce some duly authenticated document as evidence that he is such a person.

condition.p: being asked to do so

for.p: the applicant of an emergency protection order

to.p: exercise the power given by virtue of subsection (4)(b)

condition.p: the order is in force with respect to a child
this is done to safeguard the welfare of the child;

for.p: the applicant of an emergency protection order

to.p: take such action

condition.p: the order is in force with respect to a child

this is done for meeting his parental responsibility for the child as is reasonably required to safeguard or promote the welfare of the child (having regard in particular to the duration of the order).

to.p: give such directions (if any) as it considers appropriate with respect to—

ul.li: (a) the contact which is,
or is not,
to be allowed between the child and any named person;
(b) the medical or psychiatric examination or other assessment of the child.

condition.p: the court makes an emergency protection order

to.p: refuse to submit to the examination or other assessment.

condition.p: any direction is given under subsection (6)(b)
he is of sufficient understanding to make an informed decision

to.p: impose conditions and one under subsection (6)(b) may be to the effect that there is to be—

ul.li: (a) no such examination or assessment;
or (b) no such examination or assessment unless the court directs otherwise.

to.p: be—

ul.li: (a) given when the emergency protection order is made or at any time while it is in force;
and (b) varied at any time on the application of any person falling within any class of person prescribed by rules of court for the purposes of this subsection.

for.p: the applicant of an emergency protection order

to.p: return the child or (as the case may be) allow him to be removed

condition.p: the order is in force with respect to the child and—

ul.li: (a) the applicant has exercised the power given by subsection (4)(b)(i) but it appears to him that it is safe for the child to be returned;
or (b) the applicant has exercised the power given by subsection (4)(b)(ii) but it appears to him that it is safe for the child to be allowed to be removed from the place in question,

to.p: return a child to the care of the person from whose care he was removed

condition.p: he is required by subsection (10) to return the child

to.p: return a child to the care of— (i) a parent of his; (ii) any person who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him; or (iii) such other person as the applicant (with the agreement of the court) considers appropriate.

condition.p: that is not reasonably practicable

to.p: again exercise his powers with respect to the child (at any time while the emergency protection order remains in force)

condition.p: the applicant has been required by subsection (10) to return the child, or to allow him to be removed
it appears to him that a change in the circumstances of the case makes it necessary for him to do so.

to.p: allow a child reasonable contact with—

ul.li: (a) his parents;
(b) any person who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him;
(c) any person with whom he was living immediately before the making of the order;
(d) any person named in a child arrangements order as a person with whom the child is to spend time or otherwise have contact;
(e) any person who is allowed to have contact with the child by virtue of an order under section 34;
and (f) any person acting on behalf of any of those persons.

condition.p: an emergency protection order has been made with respect to the child SUBJECT TO any direction given under subsection (6)

to.p: name the child

condition.p: it is reasonably practicable to do so

to.p: describe the child as clearly as possible

condition.p: it does not name him.

to.p: intentionally obstructs any person exercising the power under subsection (4)(b) to remove, or prevent the removal of, a child.
include an exclusion requirement in an emergency protection order

ul.li: (a) on being satisfied as mentioned in section 44(1)

to.p: provide that the exclusion requirement is to have effect for a shorter period than the other provisions of the order.
attach a power of arrest to an exclusion requirement

condition.p: the court makes an emergency protection order containing an exclusion requirement

for.p: Any period specified for the purposes of subsection (4) or (6)

to.p: be extended by the court (on one or more occasions)

condition.p: the court receives an application to vary or discharge the emergency protection order.

to.p: arrest without warrant any person reasonably believed to be in breach of an exclusion requirement

condition.p: a power of arrest is attached to an exclusion requirement of an emergency protection order by virtue of subsection (5)

to.p: accept an undertaking from the relevant person

condition.p: the court has power to include an exclusion requirement in an emergency protection order

to.p: be attached to any undertaking given under subsection (1)
specify a period which ends at noon on the first later day which is not such a holiday

ul.li: (a) the court making an emergency protection order would,
but for this subsection,
specify a period of eight days as the period for which the order is to have effect;
but (b) the last of those eight days is a public holiday (that is to say,
Christmas Day,
Good Friday,
a bank holiday or a Sunday),

to.p: apply to the court for the period during which the emergency protection order is to have effect to be extended.
extend the period during which the order is to have effect by such period, not exceeding seven days, as it thinks fit

condition.p: receiving an application under subsection (4)
it has reasonable cause to believe the child is likely to suffer significant harm if the order is not extended.

to.p: only be extended once.

for.p: a court hearing an application for, or with respect to, an emergency protection order

to.p: regardless of any enactment or rule of law which would otherwise prevent it from doing so, take account of—

ul.li: (a) any statement contained in any report made to the court in the course of,
or in connection with,
the hearing;
or (b) any evidence given during the hearing,
which is,
in the opinion of the court,
relevant to the application
(a) the child;

to.p: apply to the court for an emergency protection order to be discharged

ul.li: (a) the person who would otherwise be entitled to apply for the emergency protection order to be discharged— (i) was given notice (in accordance with rules of court) of the hearing at which the order was made;
and (ii )was present at that hearing;
or (b) the effective period of the emergency protection order has been extended under subsection (5).

to.p: be varied or discharged by the court in so far as it imposes the exclusion requirement

condition.p: the court receives the application of a person who is not entitled to apply for the order to be discharged, but who is a person to whom an exclusion requirement contained in the order applies.

to.p: vary or discharge the order in so far as it confers a power of arrest (whether or not any application has been made to vary or discharge any other provision of the order)

condition.p: a power of arrest has been attached to an exclusion requirement of an emergency protection order
receiving the application of any person entitled to apply for the discharge of the order so far as it imposes the exclusion requirement

to.p: be made against—

ul.li: (a) the making of,
or refusal to make,
an emergency protection order;
(b) the extension of,
or refusal to extend,
the period during which such an order is to have effect;
(c) the discharge of,
or refusal to discharge,
such an order;
or (d) the giving of,
or refusal to give,
any direction in connection with such an order.

for.p: A court making an emergency protection order

to.p: direct that the applicant may, in exercising any powers which he has by virtue of the order, be accompanied by a registered medical practitioner, registered nurse or registered midwife, if he so chooses.

ul.li: (a) remove the child to suitable accommodation and keep him there;
or (b) take such steps as are reasonable to ensure that the child’s removal from any hospital,
or other place,
in which he is then being accommodated is prevented.

condition.p: the constable has reasonable cause to believe the child would otherwise be likely to suffer significant harm

to.p: as soon as is reasonably practicable after taking a child into police protection,

ul.li: (a) inform the local authority within whose area the child was found of the steps that have been,
and are proposed to be,
taken with respect to the child under this section and the reasons for taking them;
(b) give details to the authority within whose area the child is ordinarily resident (“the appropriate authority”) of the place at which the child is being accommodated;
(c) inform the child (if he appears capable of understanding)— (i) of the steps that have been taken with respect to him under this section and of the reasons for taking them;
and (ii) of the further steps that may be taken with respect to him under this section;
(d) take such steps as are reasonably practicable to discover the wishes and feelings of the child;
(e) secure that the case is inquired into by an officer designated for the purposes of this section by the chief officer of the police area concerned;
and (f) where the child was taken into police protection by being removed to accommodation which is not provided— (i) by or on behalf of a local authority;
or (ii) as a refuge,
in compliance with the requirements of section 51,
secure that he is moved to accommodation which is so provided

to.p: as soon as is reasonably practicable after taking a child into police protection, take such steps as are reasonably practicable to inform—

ul.li: (a) the child’s parents;
(b) every person who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him;
and (c) any other person with whom the child was living immediately before being taken into police protection,
of the steps that he has taken under this section with respect to the child,
the reasons for taking them and the further steps that may be taken with respect to him under this section.

for.p: the officer conducting any inquiry under subsection (3)(e)

to.p: release the child from police protection

condition.p: he is completing it
he considers that there is still reasonable cause for believing that the child would be likely to suffer significant harm if released.

to.p: be kept in police protection for more than 72 hours
apply on behalf of the appropriate authority for an emergency protection order to be made under section 44 with respect to a child

condition.p: the child is being kept in police protection

to.p: do what is reasonable in all the circumstances of the case for the purpose of safeguarding or promoting a child’s welfare (having regard in particular to the length of the period during which the child will be so protected)

condition.p: the child is being kept in police protection.

to.p: allow—

ul.li: (a) a child’s parents;
(b) any person who is not a parent of the child but who has parental responsibility for him;
(c) any person with whom the child was living immediately before he was taken into police protection;
(d) any person named in a child arrangements order as a person with whom the child is to spend time or otherwise have contact;
(e) any person who is allowed to have contact with the child by virtue of an order under section 34;
and (f) any person acting on behalf of any of those persons,
to have such contact (if any) with the child as,
in the opinion of the designated officer,
is both reasonable and in the child’s best interests

condition.p: the child has been taken into police protection.

to.p: make, or cause to be made, necessary enquiries to decide whether action should be taken to safeguard or promote the child’s welfare

condition.p: the local authority local—

ul.li: (a) are informed that a child who lives,
or is found,
in their area— (i) is the subject of an emergency protection order;
or (ii) is in police protection;
(b) have reasonable cause to suspect that a child who lives,
or is found,
in their area is suffering,
or is likely to suffer,
significant harm.

to.p: make, or cause to be made, such enquiries as they consider necessary to enable them to decide what action they should take to safeguard or promote a child’s welfare

condition.p: the local authority have obtained an emergency protection order with respect to the child.

to.p: be directed towards establishing—

ul.li: (a) whether the authority should— (i) make any application to court under this Act;
(ii) exercise any of their other powers under this Act;
(iii) exercise any of their powers under section 11 of the Crime and Disorder Act 1998 (child safety orders);
or (iv) (where the authority is a local authority in Wales) exercise any of their powers under the Social Services and Well-being (Wales) Act 2014;
with respect to the child;
(b) whether,
in the case of a child— (i) with respect to whom an emergency protection order has been made;
and (ii) who is not in accommodation provided by or on behalf of the authority,
it would be in the child’s best interests (while an emergency protection order remains in force) for him to be in such accommodation;
and (c) whether,
in the case of a child who has been taken into police protection,
it would be in the child’s best interests for the authority to ask for an application to be made under section 46(7).

to.p: (with a view to enabling them to determine what action, if any, to take with respect to a child) take such steps as are reasonably practicable—

ul.li: (a) to obtain access to him;
or (b) to ensure that access to him is obtained,
on their behalf,
by a person authorised by them for the purpose,

condition.p: enquiries are being made under subsection (1) with respect to the child
they are satisfied that they already have sufficient information with respect to him.

to.p: consult the local authority (as defined in section 579(1) of the Education 1996), if different, specified in subsection (5ZA).

condition.p: , as a result of any such enquiries, it appears to the authority that there are matters connected with the child’s education which should be investigated

to.p: for the purposes of making a determination under this section as to the action to be taken with respect to a child, so far as is reasonably practicable and consistent with the child’s welfare—

ul.li: (a) ascertain the child’s wishes and feelings regarding the action to be taken with respect to him;
and (b) give due consideration (having regard to his age and understanding) to such wishes and feelings of the child as they have been able to ascertain.

to.p: apply for an emergency protection order, a child assessment order, a care order or a supervision order with respect to the child

condition.p: , in the course of enquiries made under this section—

ul.li: (a) any officer of the local authority concerned;
or (b) any person authorised by the authority to act on their behalf in connection with those enquiries— (i) is refused access to the child concerned;
or (ii) is denied information as to his whereabouts,

condition.p: they are satisfied that his welfare can be satisfactorily safeguarded without their doing so.

ul.li: (a) consider whether it would be appropriate to review the case at a later date;
and (b) if they decide that it would be,
determine the date on which that review is to begin

condition.p: , on the conclusion of any enquiries or review made under this section, the authority decide not to apply for an emergency protection order, a child assessment order, a care order or a supervision order.

to.p: take that action (so far as it is both within their power and reasonably practicable for them to do so)

condition.p: , as a result of complying with this section, a local authority conclude that they should take action to safeguard or promote the child’s welfare.

to.p: assist a local authority with those enquiries (in particular by providing relevant information and advice)

condition.p: the local authority are conducting enquiries under this section
called upon by the authority to do so.

to.p: consult that other authority, who may undertake the necessary enquiries in their place

condition.p: the local authority are making enquiries under this section with respect to a child who appears to them to be ordinarily resident within the area of another authority.

to.p: include in an emergency protection order a provision requiring that other person to disclose any information that he may have as to the child’s whereabouts

condition.p: it appears to the court making the order that adequate information as to the child’s whereabouts—

ul.li: (a) is not available to the applicant for the order;
but (b) is available to another person,

condition.p: if asked to do so by the applicant.

to.p: comply with such a requirement

condition.p: complying might incriminate him or his spouse or civil partner of an offence.

to.p: authorise the applicant to enter premises specified by the order and search for the child with respect to whom the order is made.
make an order authorising the applicant of an emergency protection order to search for that other child on those premises

condition.p: the court is satisfied that there is reasonable cause to believe that there may be another child on those premises with respect to whom the order ought to be made.

to.p: notify the court of its effect

condition.p: the order has been made under subsection (4).

to.p: obstruct any person exercising the power of entry and search under subsection (3) or (4).
issue a warrant authorising any constable to assist the person mentioned in paragraph

ul.li: (a) or (b) in the exercise of those powers using reasonable force

condition.p: , on an application made by any person for a warrant under this section, it appears to the court—

ul.li: (a)that a person attempting to exercise powers under an emergency protection order has been prevented from doing so by being refused entry to the premises concerned or access to the child concerned;
or (b)that any such person is likely to be so prevented from exercising any such powers

condition.p: necessary

for.p: Every warrant issued under this section

to.p: be addressed to, and executed by, a constable who shall be accompanied by the person applying for the warrant

ul.li: (a) that person so desires;
and (b) the court by whom the warrant is issued does not direct otherwise.

for.p: A court granting an application for a warrant under this section

to.p: direct that the constable concerned may, in executing the warrant, be accompanied by a registered medical practitioner, registered nurse or registered midwife if he so chooses.

for.p: An application for a warrant under this section

to.p: be made in the manner and form prescribed by rules of court.

for.p: an order under subsection (4), an application for a warrant under this section and any such warrant

to.p: name the child

condition.p: it is reasonably practicable to do so, an order under subsection (4).

for.p: an order under subsection (4), an application for a warrant under this section and any such warrant

to.p: describe the child as clearly as possible

condition.p: it does not name him.

to.p: knowingly and without lawful authority or reasonable excuse,

ul.li: (a) takes a child to whom this section applies away from the responsible person;
(b) keeps such a child away from the responsible person;
or (c) induces,
assists or incites such a child to run away or stay away from the responsible person.

to.p: make an order under this section (“a recovery order”)

condition.p: it appears to the court that there is reason to believe that a child to whom this section applies—

ul.li: (a) has been unlawfully taken away or is being unlawfully kept away from the responsible person;
(b) has run away or is staying away from the responsible person;
or (c) is missing,

to.p: make a recovery order

condition.p: it receives the application of—

ul.li: (a) any person who has parental responsibility for the child by virtue of a care order or emergency protection order;
or (b) where the child is in police protection,
the designated officer.

to.p: name the child and—

ul.li: (a) any person who has parental responsibility for the child by virtue of a care order or emergency protection order;
or (b) where the child is in police protection,
the designated officer.

to.p: be specified under subsection (3)(d)

condition.p: it appears to the court that there are reasonable grounds for believing the child to be on them.

to.p: identify the recovery order

condition.p: a person is authorised as mentioned in subsection (7)(c).

for.p: any person claiming to be authorised

to.p: produce some duly authenticated document showing that he is so authorised

condition.p: a person is authorised as mentioned in subsection (7)(c)
asked to do so

to.p: intentionally obstruct an authorised person exercising the power under subsection (3)(b) to remove a child.
comply with any request made under subsection (3)(c) on the ground that complying with it might incriminate him or his spouse or civil partner of an offence
issue a certificate under this section with respect to that home

condition.p: it is proposed to use a voluntary home or private children’s home to provide a refuge for children who appear to be at risk of harm.

to.p: issue a certificate under this section with respect to that foster parent

condition.p: a local authority or voluntary organisation arrange for a foster parent to provide such a refuge.

to.p: by regulations—

ul.li: (a) make provision as to the manner in which certificates may be issued;
(b) impose requirements which must be complied with while any certificate is in force;
and (c) provide for the withdrawal of certificates in prescribed circumstances.

to.p: Without prejudice to section 93 or any other power to make such rules, be made with respect to the procedure to be followed in connection with proceedings under this Part.
in particular make provision—

ul.li: (a) as to the form in which any application is to be made or direction is to be given;
(b) prescribing the persons who are to be notified of— (i) the making,
or extension,
of an emergency protection order;
or (ii) the making of an application under section 45(4) or (8) or 46(7);
and (c) as to the content of any such notification and the manner in which,
and person by whom,
it is to be given.

to.p: by regulations provide that the authority shall be treated, for the purposes of this Act, as though they and not the original applicant had applied for, and been granted, the order

ul.li: (a) an emergency protection order has been made with respect to a child;
(b) the applicant for the order was not the local authority within whose area the child is ordinarily resident;
and (c) that local authority are of the opinion that it would be in the child’s best interests for the applicant’s responsibilities under the order to be transferred to them,

condition.p: their having complied with any requirements imposed by the regulations.

to.p: in particular, make provision as to—

ul.li: (a) the considerations to which the local authority shall have regard in forming an opinion as mentioned in subsection (3)(c);
and (b) the time at which responsibility under any emergency protection order is to be treated as having been transferred to a local authority.

to.p: make such arrangements as they consider appropriate for securing that homes (“community homes”) are available—

ul.li: (a) for the care and accommodation of children looked after by them;
and (b) for purposes connected with the welfare of children (whether or not looked after by them),

to.p: do so jointly with one or more other local authorities.
have regard to the need for ensuring the availability of accommodation—

ul.li: (a) of different descriptions;
and (b) which is suitable for different purposes and the requirements of different descriptions of children

condition.p: making such arrangements.

to.p: make arrangements for the management by another person of accommodation provided by the local authority for the purpose of restricting the liberty of children.

with the consent of the body of managers constituted by the instrument of management for the home, make arrangements for the management by another person of accommodation provided for the purpose of restricting the liberty of children

condition.p: the local authority are to be responsible for the management of a community home provided by a voluntary organisation.

to.p: designate the home as a controlled community home

condition.p: the local authority are to be responsible for the management of a community home provided by a voluntary organisation.

to.p: designate the home as an assisted community home

condition.p: a voluntary organisation are to be responsible for the management of a community home provided by the organisation.

for.p: any dispute relating to a controlled community home

to.p: be referred by either party to the Secretary of State for his determination

condition.p: the dispute arises between the local authority specified in the home’s instrument of management and—

ul.li: (a) the voluntary organisation by which the home is provided;
or (b) any other local authority who have placed,
or desire or are required to place,
in the home a child who is looked after by them,

for.p: any dispute relating to an assisted community home

to.p: be referred by either party to the Secretary of State for his determination

condition.p: the dispute arises between the voluntary organisation by which the home is provided and any local authority who have placed, or desire to place, in the home a child who is looked after by them.

to.p: in order to give effect to his determination of a dispute, give such directions as he thinks fit to the local authority or voluntary organisation concerned

condition.p: the dispute is referred to the Secretary of State under this section.

for.p: a dispute which is capable of being dealt with in accordance with a provision whereby a bishop or any other ecclesiastical or denominational authority has power to decide questions relating to religious instruction given in the home

to.p: be referred to the Secretary of State under this section

condition.p: any trust deed relating to a controlled or assisted community home contains that provision.

for.p: The voluntary organisation by which a controlled or assisted community home is provided

to.p: cease to provide the home

condition.p: they have given to the Secretary of State and to the local authority specified in the home’s instrument of management not less than two years’ notice in writing of their intention to do so.

to.p: specify the date from which the voluntary organisation intend to cease to provide the home as a community home.
by order—

ul.li: (a) revoke the home’s instrument of management;
and (b) require the local authority who were specified in that instrument to conduct the home until— (i) the date specified in the subsection (1) notice;
or (ii) such earlier date (if any) as may be specified for the purposes of this paragraph in the order,
as if it were a community home provided by the local authority

condition.p: a notice is given under subsection (1) and the home’s managers give notice in writing to the Secretary of State that they are unable or unwilling to continue as its managers until the date specified in the subsection (1) notice.

for.p: something in the trust deed for the home

to.p: affect the conduct of the home by the local authority

condition.p: the Secretary of State imposes a requirement under subsection (4)(b).

to.p: by order direct that for the purposes of any provision specified in the direction and made by or under any enactment relating to community homes (other than this section) the home shall, until the date or earlier date specified as mentioned in subsection (4)(b), be treated as a controlled or assisted community home

condition.p: the Secretary of State imposes a requirement under subsection (4)(b).

to.p: until that date be treated for the purposes of any such enactment as a community home provided by the local authority

condition.p: the Secretary of State imposes a requirement under subsection (4)(b)
the Secretary of State directs otherwise.

for.p: The local authority specified in the instrument of management for a controlled or assisted community home

to.p: give—

ul.li: (a) the Secretary of State;
and (b) the voluntary organisation by which the home is provided,
not less than two years’ notice in writing of their intention to withdraw their designation of the home as a controlled or assisted community home.

to.p: specify the date (“the specified date”) on which the designation is to be withdrawn.
by order revoke the home’s instrument of management from such date earlier than the specified date as may be specified in the order

ul.li: (a) a notice is given under subsection (1) in respect of a controlled or assisted community home;
(b) the home’s managers give notice in writing to the Secretary of State that they are unable or unwilling to continue as managers until the specified date;
and (c) the managers’ notice is not withdrawn.

to.p: consult the local authority and voluntary organisation

condition.p: making an order under subsection (3).

to.p: be paid—

ul.li: (a) in the case of compensation payable under subsection (2),
to the authority who were the responsible authority at the relevant time;
and (b) in any other case,
to the Secretary of State.

for.p: The amount of any sum payable under this section

to.p: be determined in accordance with such arrangements—

ul.li: (a) as may be agreed between the voluntary organisation by which the home is,
at the relevant time,
provided and the responsible authority or (as the case may be) the Secretary of State;
or (b) in default of agreement,
as may be determined by the Secretary of State.

for.p: the liability to pay any sum under this section

to.p: With the agreement of the responsible authority or (as the case may be) the Secretary of State, be discharged, in whole or in part, by the transfer of any premises.
